# Safeparts

LLM integration + maintenance guide for this repository.
This is not end-user documentation. It exists to help an LLM locate the right code, use the APIs correctly, and make safe changes.

If you only read one section, read "Non-negotiable safety rules".

## Non-negotiable safety rules
- Treat share strings, SharePacket bytes, reconstructed secrets, ciphertext, and passphrases as secrets.
- Do not ask for, paste, or log real secrets/shares/passphrases.
- In examples and tests, use synthetic inputs ("hello", random bytes created locally).
- Avoid printing data that could contain secrets (including debug dumps of packets).
- Do not commit generated build output (notably `web/src/wasm_pkg/`, `target/`, `web/dist/`).
- This workspace forbids `unsafe` code (see `Cargo.toml`). Do not add `unsafe`.
- Avoid `unwrap()`/`expect()` in non-test code; return errors instead.
- Preserve zeroization patterns (`zeroize`, `Zeroizing`) when touching code that handles secrets/passphrases.

## What this repo ships
- Rust library for integration: `safeparts_core` (`crates/safeparts_core/`)
- CLI: `safeparts` (`crates/safeparts/`)
- TUI: `safeparts-tui` (`crates/safeparts_tui/`) + `safeparts tui` launcher
- WASM bindings: `safeparts_wasm` (`crates/safeparts_wasm/`)
- Web UI: `web/` (Vite + React; split/combine runs locally in the browser via WASM)
- Help site: `web/help/` (Astro + Starlight; served under `/help/` and built into `web/dist/help/`)

Repo map (quick)
- Core logic: `crates/safeparts_core/src/`
- CLI: `crates/safeparts/src/main.rs`
- TUI: `crates/safeparts_tui/src/app.rs` + `crates/safeparts_tui/src/domain.rs`
- WASM exports: `crates/safeparts_wasm/src/lib.rs`
- Web UI: `web/src/`
- CI truth: `.github/workflows/`

Secondary references (useful context, but code is the source of truth)
- Repo overview: `AGENTS.md`
- Quickstart: `README.md`
- Intent + threat model: `PRD.md`
- CI-style local commands: `docs/agents/checks.md`
- Repo conventions: `docs/agents/conventions.md`
- End-user docs content: `web/help/src/content/docs/`

## The core model (from code)

Split flow
- Input is arbitrary bytes.
- Optional: encrypt the secret first using Argon2id -> ChaCha20-Poly1305.
  - Current defaults (see `crates/safeparts_core/src/crypto.rs`): mem_cost_kib=65536, time_cost=3, parallelism=1; salt=16 bytes; nonce=12 bytes.
- Compute integrity tag = BLAKE3(data_to_split) and append it (32 bytes).
- Shamir split the tagged bytes over GF(256), byte-wise, into n shares.
- Wrap each share into a versioned SharePacket that carries metadata.
- Encode SharePacket bytes for transport (base64url/base58check/mnemonics).

Combine flow
- Decode share strings into SharePackets.
- Validate the share set is consistent (same set_id/k/n/x/payload length; no duplicate x).
- Validate crypto params are consistent across packets.
- Shamir combine to reconstruct the tagged bytes.
- Verify integrity tag before decrypting.
- If encrypted: decrypt using passphrase and the per-packet crypto params.
- Output recovered secret bytes.

Where this logic lives
- Split/combine API: `crates/safeparts_core/src/lib.rs`
- Shamir split/combine: `crates/safeparts_core/src/sss.rs` + `crates/safeparts_core/src/gf256.rs`
- Share packet format: `crates/safeparts_core/src/packet.rs`
- Encodings: `crates/safeparts_core/src/ascii.rs`, `crates/safeparts_core/src/mnemo_words.rs`, `crates/safeparts_core/src/mnemo_bip39.rs`
- Optional encryption: `crates/safeparts_core/src/crypto.rs`

Constraints and edge cases
- Parameters are bytes: `1 <= k <= n <= 255` (type is `u8`).
- `k=1` is allowed by the core but is a degenerate scheme (any single share reveals the secret).
- `x=0` is invalid; generated shares use `x = 1..=n`.

## Data structures and formats

### SharePacket (the real share format)
Type: `crates/safeparts_core/src/packet.rs`

In memory
- `SharePacket { set_id, k, n, x, payload, crypto_params }`
- `set_id` is 16 random bytes identifying the share set.
- `payload` is the per-share y-bytes (same length across shares in a set).
- `crypto_params` is present when encrypt-then-split was used.

Binary layout (conceptual)
```text
magic      : "SMN1"
version    : u8
flags      : u8   (bit 0 = encrypted)
k, n, x    : u8, u8, u8
set_id     : [u8; 16]

if encrypted:
  salt         : [u8; 16]
  nonce        : [u8; 12]
  mem_cost_kib : u32 (BE)
  time_cost    : u32 (BE)
  parallelism  : u32 (BE)

payload_len: u32 (BE)
payload    : bytes
```

Versioning
- Encode uses V2 today; decode supports V1 and V2.
- If you change this format, add a new version and keep old decode working.

### Encodings (transport, not semantics)
All encodings are reversible wrappers around SharePacket bytes.

- Base64url (`ascii::Encoding::Base64url`)
  - URL-safe, no padding.
  - No checksum beyond SharePacket parsing and the integrity tag.
- Base58check (`ascii::Encoding::Base58check`)
  - Includes a checksum.
- Mnemo-words (`mnemo_words`)
  - Uses the BIP-39 English word list as an alphabet.
  - Adds a CRC16 (CCITT-FALSE) over a framed payload.
  - Decode is case-tolerant.
- Mnemo-bip39 (`mnemo_bip39`)
  - Encodes SharePacket bytes into one or more valid BIP-39 mnemonics.
  - A share may be multiple phrases separated by '/'.
  - Decode is case-tolerant and rejects missing/duplicate frames.

Important: mnemonic outputs are an encoding for share packets, not wallet seed phrases.

## Public surfaces

### Rust library: `safeparts_core`
Primary entry point: `crates/safeparts_core/src/lib.rs`

Modules
- `ascii`: base64url/base58check encoding of SharePacket bytes
- `mnemo_words`: word-based encoding + CRC16
- `mnemo_bip39`: BIP-39-valid encoding (multi-frame)
- `packet`: SharePacket binary format
- `sss`: Shamir split/combine over GF(256)
- `crypto`: Argon2id + ChaCha20-Poly1305
- `error`: `CoreError`, `CoreResult`

Key functions
- `split_secret(secret: &[u8], k: u8, n: u8, passphrase: Option<&[u8]>) -> CoreResult<Vec<SharePacket>>`
- `combine_shares(packets: &[SharePacket], passphrase: Option<&[u8]>) -> CoreResult<Vec<u8>>`
- Convenience aliases:
  - `tag_and_split(secret, k, n)` (same as `split_secret(..., None)`)
  - `combine_and_verify(packets)` (same as `combine_shares(..., None)`)

Errors you should handle
- `NotEnoughShares { k, m }`: fewer than k shares.
- `InconsistentMetadata`: mixed sets or inconsistent lengths.
- `DuplicateX { x }` / `InvalidX`: bad share indices.
- `IntegrityCheckFailed`: wrong shares or corrupted shares.
- `PassphraseRequired`: share set is encrypted but passphrase not provided.
- `DecryptFailed`: wrong passphrase or tampered ciphertext.
- `CryptoParamsMismatch`: mixed encrypted/unencrypted shares or mismatched crypto params.

Rust integration example (encode shares as base64url strings)
```rust
use safeparts_core::{ascii, combine_shares, packet::SharePacket, split_secret, CoreResult};

fn main() -> CoreResult<()> {
    let packets = split_secret(b"secret", 2, 3, None)?;

    // Store/transport as strings.
    let encoded: Vec<String> = packets
        .iter()
        .map(|p| ascii::encode_packet(p, ascii::Encoding::Base64url))
        .collect::<CoreResult<_>>()?;

    // Later: decode strings back into SharePackets.
    let decoded: Vec<SharePacket> = encoded
        .iter()
        .take(2)
        .map(|s| ascii::decode_packet(s, ascii::Encoding::Base64url))
        .collect::<CoreResult<_>>()?;

    let recovered = combine_shares(&decoded, None)?;
    assert_eq!(recovered, b"secret");
    Ok(())
}
```

### CLI: `safeparts`
Implementation: `crates/safeparts/src/main.rs`

Commands
- `safeparts split -k <K> -n <N> [-e <encoding>] [-p <pass>] [-P <file>] [-i <file>] [-o <file>]`
- `safeparts combine [-e <encoding>] [-p <pass>] [-P <file>] [-i <file>] [-o <file>]`
- `safeparts tui` (launcher for `safeparts-tui`)

Flag alias note
- `combine --encoding` also accepts `--from`.

I/O rules
- `split` reads raw bytes from stdin by default; it does not interpret UTF-8.
- `combine` reads share text as UTF-8.
- `combine` writes recovered bytes to stdout/file.
- `-i -` and `-o -` explicitly select stdin/stdout.

Encoding flags (CLI)
- `base64` (alias `base64url`)
- `base58` (alias `base58check`)
- `mnemo-words`
- `mnemo-bip39`

Passphrase flags (CLI)
- `-p/--passphrase <text>` or `-P/--passphrase-file <path>` (file input trims trailing `\n`/`\r`).
- Prefer `--passphrase-file` in real usage to avoid shell history.

Auto-detect (CLI combine)
- If any non-empty line contains '/' or has multiple words, treat input as mnemonic.
  - If any line contains '/', decode as mnemo-bip39.
  - Otherwise decode as mnemo-words.
- Otherwise, try base64url decode first; if that fails, try base58check.

Mnemonic parsing (CLI combine)
- Mnemonic shares may be pasted as blank-line separated blocks (recommended).
  - Each block is one share.
  - Lines within a block are joined with spaces before decoding.
- If there are multiple non-empty lines and no blank lines, the CLI treats each line as a separate share.

Tests that define the CLI contract
- `crates/safeparts/tests/e2e.rs`

### TUI: `safeparts-tui`
Entrypoint: `crates/safeparts_tui/src/main.rs`

Core logic
- UI + key handling: `crates/safeparts_tui/src/app.rs`
- Encoding and parsing rules: `crates/safeparts_tui/src/domain.rs`

Share parsing rules (TUI)
- Base64/Base58: whitespace-separated tokens.
- Mnemonics: one share per paragraph (blank-line separated); line wrapping is normalized.

Keybindings (high signal)
- `Ctrl+Q`: quit
- `F1` or `?`: help
- `Tab` / `Shift+Tab`: focus next/prev
- `Left` / `Right`: switch tabs
- `Enter`: run the focused action
- `Ctrl+L`: load (context-sensitive)
- `Ctrl+S`: save (context-sensitive)
- `Ctrl+C`: copy (context-sensitive)
- `Ctrl+V`: paste (context-sensitive)
- Passphrase field: `Ctrl+U` clears

Clipboard
- Uses system clipboard when available, otherwise OSC52 (`crates/safeparts_tui/src/clipboard.rs`).

### WASM: `safeparts_wasm`
Implementation: `crates/safeparts_wasm/src/lib.rs`

Exported functions
- `split_secret(secret: &[u8], k: u8, n: u8, encoding: &str, passphrase: Option<String>) -> Array`
- `combine_shares(shares: Array, encoding: &str, passphrase: Option<String>) -> Uint8Array`
- `share_threshold(share: &str, encoding: &str) -> u8`
- `inspect_share(share: &str, encoding: &str) -> { k, n, x, encrypted }`

Encoding strings (WASM/web)
- `base64url`
- `base58check`
- `mnemo-words`
- `mnemo-bip39`
These are not the same strings as the CLI flags. Do not mix them.

Minimal JS usage pattern
```js
// wasm-pack output exports a default init() function.
import init, * as sp from "./wasm_pkg/safeparts_wasm.js";

await init();
const secret = new TextEncoder().encode("secret");
const shares = sp.split_secret(secret, 2, 3, "base64url", null);
const recoveredBytes = sp.combine_shares([shares[0], shares[1]], "base64url", null);
const recovered = new TextDecoder().decode(recoveredBytes);
```

### Web UI
- App entry: `web/src/App.tsx`
- WASM loader: `web/src/wasm.ts` (dynamic import from `web/src/wasm_pkg/`)
- Split UI: `web/src/components/SplitForm.tsx`
- Combine UI: `web/src/components/CombineForm.tsx`

Important limitations (current behavior)
- The web UI currently treats the secret as UTF-8 text.
  - Split uses `TextEncoder().encode(secretString)`.
  - Combine uses `TextDecoder().decode(bytes)`.
- The web UI only exposes `base64url` and `mnemo-words` in the encoding selector.
  - This is enforced by the `Encoding` union in `web/src/components/ui/encoding-selector.tsx`.

Combine UI share parsing nuance
- Each share box is intended to hold one share.
- A single share box can also contain multiple shares if they are separated by blank lines.
- Base64url shares must be a single token (whitespace inside a share will break decoding).

Combine UI ergonomics (useful when debugging)
- It heuristically detects encoding from pasted text and may auto-switch the selected encoding.
- It may auto-add more share boxes by inspecting the first pasted share's threshold (`k`) via WASM.

Generated artifacts
- `web/src/wasm_pkg/` is produced by `wasm-pack build`.
- Regenerate it via `bun run build:wasm` (see `web/package.json`).
- Do not hand-edit or commit generated files in `web/src/wasm_pkg/`.

Build pipeline notes (forks)
- `web/package.json` includes a `build:wasm` script that builds `crates/safeparts_wasm/` into `web/src/wasm_pkg/`.
- Netlify build settings and redirects live in `netlify.toml`.

Docs integration (web)
- Help base URL can be overridden via `VITE_HELP_URL` (see `web/src/App.tsx`).
- In dev, Vite proxies `/help` to the docs dev server (see `web/vite.config.mjs`).

### Help site (Astro/Starlight)
- Source: `web/help/`
- Content: `web/help/src/content/docs/`
- Route map: `web/help/DOCS_MAP.md`

## Build, tests, CI

Rust (matches `.github/workflows/rust-ci.yml`)
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-features`

Web + docs + e2e (matches `.github/workflows/web-ci.yml`)
- From `web/`:
  - `bun install`
  - `bun run build:wasm`
  - `bun run build`
- From `web/help/`:
  - `bun install`
  - `bun run build`
- Web tests (from `web/`):
  - `bun run test:e2e:smoke`
  - `bun run test:e2e:full`

Where tests live
- CLI e2e: `crates/safeparts/tests/e2e.rs`
- Web tests: `web/tests/`

## Safe change recipes

Add a new encoding end-to-end
1. Core encode/decode: `crates/safeparts_core/src/`
2. CLI mapping + auto-detect (if relevant): `crates/safeparts/src/main.rs`
3. TUI enum + parsing/detect: `crates/safeparts_tui/src/domain.rs`
4. WASM string mapping: `crates/safeparts_wasm/src/lib.rs`
5. Web UI exposure (optional):
   - `web/src/components/ui/encoding-selector.tsx` (type union)
   - `web/src/components/SplitForm.tsx` and `web/src/components/CombineForm.tsx` (options + UX)
6. Tests:
   - round-trip tests in the encoding module
   - CLI e2e coverage in `crates/safeparts/tests/e2e.rs`

Change SharePacket format
- Add a new version constant.
- Keep old decode paths working.
- Add binary round-trip tests.
- Do not silently reinterpret bytes on decode.

Change crypto defaults
- Defaults: `crates/safeparts_core/src/crypto.rs` (`CryptoParams::random_default`).
- Params are stored per share packet, so changing defaults affects only new splits.
- Keep decrypt compatible with older params.

Add binary-secret support to the web UI
- Today the web UI treats secrets as UTF-8 strings.
- To support arbitrary bytes, add file input/output and avoid `TextDecoder()` for recovered bytes.
- Keep the default UX simple; do not accidentally display binary as lossy text.

## Common pitfalls
- Mixing share sets: different `set_id`/k/n/payload length will fail combine.
- Mixing encrypted and unencrypted shares: `CryptoParamsMismatch`.
- Confusing encoding names: CLI uses `base64` but WASM/web uses `base64url`.
- Base64url shares must be one token per share; whitespace inside a share breaks decode.
- `k=1` is valid but provides no threshold secrecy.

## Release packaging
- Local release-style archives are built via `scripts/release/package.py`.
- Release archives include CLI/TUI binaries; the web UI is not packaged (see `scripts/release/README.md`).

## Ignore / low-signal directories
- `target/`: Rust build output
- `web/dist/`: web build output
- `web/src/wasm_pkg/`: generated by wasm-pack
- `mobile/`: currently not a supported deliverable (looks like only `node_modules/`)
- `openspec/` and `.opencode/`: spec/tooling; ignore unless the task is explicitly about specs

## Primary sources (when in doubt)
- Core: `crates/safeparts_core/src/lib.rs`
- Packet format: `crates/safeparts_core/src/packet.rs`
- CLI: `crates/safeparts/src/main.rs`
- WASM: `crates/safeparts_wasm/src/lib.rs`
- Web UI: `web/src/components/SplitForm.tsx`, `web/src/components/CombineForm.tsx`
- CI: `.github/workflows/`
